world.title: Gen Z and Millennial readers are redefining book clubs.
world.description: Gen Z and Millennial readers are redefining book clubs. Why stop at a monthly discussion when they could eventize reading like they do for everything else? Young people are leading bookstore hops, walking book clubs, and even silent reading parties for the ones just looking for a communal hobby space without the social pressure. Literary walking clubs—where they take a stroll with a featured author, then shop discounted prices at a bookstore—are taking over the young social scene especially in Los Angeles (where everyone is posting about it). These events are not just opportunities for discussion about books, though. Young women especially treat these meetups as a chance to create real friendship over shared interests. Our data shows that reading is a top hobby among 13-39-year-olds—driven by young women—so it’s no wonder that they’re rebranding ways to connect over their love for reading IRL. (LA Times) 👀 Read more from YPulse: Gen Z’s Top 10 Biggest Hobbies Right Now

science.title: PanSTARRS comet: Rare celestial event set to light up skies - here’s how and when to watch
science.description: Comet C/2025 R3 (PanSTARRS) is emerging as one of the most anticipated skywatching events of 2026. Discovered in September 2025 by Pan-STARRS, the comet will be brightening as it approaches Earth and the Sun. With peak visibility expected in April, including a close approach to Earth, the comet may be visible to the naked eye under ideal conditions. Stargazers are advised to look toward the eastern sky before sunrise for the best chance to witness this rare celestial event.
